# Rayforce

Rayforce is an embeddable columnar analytics and graph traversal engine written in pure C17 with zero external dependencies. It combines morsel-driven vectorized execution, a multi-pass query optimizer, and a native CSR graph engine in a single pipeline. Rayforce is queried through the Rayfall language, exposes a C API for embedding, and targets Linux, macOS, and Windows (WSL) environments.

License: MIT. Author: Anton Kundenko. Source: https://github.com/RayforceDB/rayforce

## Getting Started

- [Quick Start](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/quick-start.html): Build from source, start the REPL, create a table, and run a first query.
- [Tutorial](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/tutorial.html): Guided walkthrough of core capabilities.
- [Analytics Tutorial](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/tutorial-analytics.html)
- [Graph Tutorial](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/tutorial-graph.html)
- [Datalog Tutorial](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/tutorial-datalog.html)
- [Storage Tutorial](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/tutorial-storage.html)

## Rayfall Language

- [Syntax](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/rayfall-syntax.html): Functional query language with S-expression syntax.
- [Functions Reference](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/rayfall-functions.html): All built-in functions.
- [Math Operations](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/operations-math.html)
- [String Operations](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/operations-string.html)
- [Control Flow](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/control-flow.html)
- [REPL Reference](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/repl.html)
- [All Functions Reference](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/reference.html)

## Data and Types

- [Data Types](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/data-types.html)
- [Collections](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/data-types-collections.html)

## Queries

- [Select Queries](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/queries-select.html)
- [Joins](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/queries-joins.html)
- [Linked Columns](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/queries-links.html)
- [Indexes Overview](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/indexes-overview.html)
- [Accelerator Indexes](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/queries-indexes.html)
- [Pivot](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/queries-pivot.html)
- [Vector Search](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/vector-search.html)

## Storage and IPC

- [Columnar Storage](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/storage.html)
- [IPC and Serialization](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/ipc.html)

## Graph Engine

- [Graph Storage](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/graph-storage.html)
- [Graph Algorithms](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/graph-algorithms.html): BFS, DFS, Dijkstra, A*, PageRank, Louvain, betweenness, worst-case optimal joins.

## Architecture

- [Pipeline and Optimizer](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/architecture-pipeline.html): Three-phase pipeline — DAG construction, multi-pass optimization, morsel-driven execution.
- [Memory Model](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/architecture-memory.html): Custom buddy allocator with slab caches, per-thread heaps, zero-malloc query execution.
- [Block Offloading](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/architecture-offloading.html)

## C API

- [Core API](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/c-api-core.html): Embed Rayforce in C applications via the single header `rayforce.h`.
- [DAG API](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/c-api-dag.html): Programmatic query graph construction.

- [Indexes Guide](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/guide-indexes.html)
- [Analytics Guide](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/guide-analytics.html)
- [Datalog Guide](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/guide-datalog.html)
- [Datalog Reference](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/datalog.html)
- [Storage Guide](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/guide-storage.html)
- [IPC Guide](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/guide-ipc.html)
- [Error Handling](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/guide-errors.html)
- [Memory and Monitoring](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/guide-memory.html)
- [Type Casting](https://core.rayforcedb.com/docs/guide-types.html)
